scope.summary: B10 makes the setup-printed Claude HTTP onboarding snippet carry an Authorization header and proves it through the C9 snippet-truth lane.
out_of_scope: No change is made to HTTP authentication policy; unauthenticated /mcp remains refused unless explicit no-auth dev mode is selected.
No bearer value is committed; the C9 lane stores only redacted clients issue output.

entry_points.name: C9 snippet-truth lane
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 900 -- bash scripts/rig/onboarding_snippet_truth.sh run --log passed at source 1b969d340c69acf0bafebfea5308c001a18a6f53. Artifact target/e2e/c9-snippet-truth-20260722T094018Z-2743004/summary.json records http_client_credentials.claude_mcp_add status=pass after extracting the real setup output and completing raw HTTP initialize with the issued bearer.

entry_points.name: setup payload regression
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp setup_payload_is_generic_and_client_ready -- --nocapture passed: setup JSON includes claude_mcp_add with URL plus --header Authorization: Bearer <bearer>, and rendered text quotes the header as one shell argument.

entry_points.name: clients issue client_command regression
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp client_credential_commands_distinguish_offline_and_live_revocation -- --nocapture passed: clients issue client_command carries the same HTTP URL plus Authorization header shape.

entry_points.name: scoped compile and format
entry_points.trace: cargo fmt --all -- --check and env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo check -p oraclemcp both passed after the B10 source changes.

entry_points.name: C9 harness syntax
entry_points.trace: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m py_compile scripts/rig/onboarding_snippet_truth.py and bash -n scripts/rig/onboarding_snippet_truth.sh both passed before rerunning the lane.

limitations: Scoped local evidence only; no full-workspace gate is claimed from this shared dirty checkout.

The setup snippet necessarily carries a <bearer> placeholder because setup does not mutate client credentials; C9 substitutes the shown-once bearer emitted by the real clients issue command in memory and stores only redacted output.
